title: lastrea filix-mas subsp. borreri
intro: Lastrea filix-mas subsp. borreri, also known as the Borrer's lady-fern, is a subspecies of the lady fern (Lastrea filix-mas) that belongs to the Osmundaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lastrea filix-mas subsp. borreri include reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and aiding in the treatment of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and it can also be dried and used in potpourri or as a decorative element.
